payload.subject: [MVP-E1] Foundation & Integrations
payload.description:
Source backlog key: MVP-E1

Secure startup and provider adapters

Why
Reliability depends on trustworthy data flow

In scope
- Config
- auth
- identity normalization
- Jira/GitHub adapters

Out of scope
- OAuth multi-user
- DB
- background sync

Acceptance criteria
- Children complete; known user yields normalized payloads; missing config blocks startup

Dependencies: None
Priority: P0
Estimate: 8h roll-up
Owner role: Tech Lead / Integrations
Risk level: High
Demo relevance: Critical
Suggested status: Ready

Artifacts / files expected
- src/config/*
- src/adapters/*
- config/*.json

Test notes
Validated by child tickets and fixtures

Rollback / fallback
Use fixtures or single-provider demo

payload.subject: [MVP-E2] Query & Experience
payload.description:
Source backlog key: MVP-E2

Natural language to grounded answer

Why
This is the visible AI layer

In scope
- Parsing
- orchestration
- rendering
- web UI
- thin CLI

Out of scope
- Chat history
- session memory
- agent autonomy

Acceptance criteria
- End-to-end answer works; partial failures visible; web path demoable

Dependencies: MVP-E1
Priority: P0
Estimate: 7h roll-up
Owner role: Tech Lead / Full-stack
Risk level: High
Demo relevance: Critical
Suggested status: Ready

Artifacts / files expected
- src/query/*
- src/orchestrator/*
- src/render/*
- public/*

Test notes
Smoke questions and grounding checks

Rollback / fallback
"Web first, CLI minimal"

payload.subject: [MVP-E3] Quality Ops & Demo
payload.description:
Source backlog key: MVP-E3

Harden for review and demo

Why
Reviewer trust depends on reliability and clarity

In scope
- Tests
- logs
- docs
- fallback data
- tracker exports

Out of scope
- CI/CD
- cloud deploy

Acceptance criteria
- Fixtures pass; fallback mode works; setup docs are reviewer-friendly

Dependencies: MVP-E1, MVP-E2
Priority: P1
Estimate: 4h roll-up
Owner role: QA / PM / Presenter
Risk level: Medium
Demo relevance: Critical
Suggested status: Ready

Artifacts / files expected
- README.md
- planning/*
- fixtures/*

Test notes
Smoke plus negative-path rehearsal

Rollback / fallback
Run recorded demo path if live APIs degrade

operations.subject: [MVP-01] Bootstrap env schema and auth guardrails

payload.subject: [MVP-01] Bootstrap env schema and auth guardrails
payload.description:
Source backlog key: MVP-01

Create TS runtime and fail-fast config

Why
Prevents lost time on bad tokens and missing scope

In scope
- Express server
- env validation
- sample configs
- auth smoke

Out of scope
- OAuth login
- secret manager

Acceptance criteria
- Dev server starts; health OK; missing env fails clearly; sample config documented

Dependencies: MVP-E1
Priority: P0
Estimate: 1.5h
Owner role: Platform / Integrations
Risk level: Medium
Demo relevance: High
Suggested status: Ready

Artifacts / files expected
- package.json
- tsconfig.json
- src/server.ts
- src/config.ts
- .env.example
- config/*.example.json

Test notes
Env validation unit test and health smoke

Rollback / fallback
Boot in fixture mode if live auth fails

operations.subject: [MVP-02] Team identity mapping query parsing and timeframe rules

payload.subject: [MVP-02] Team identity mapping query parsing and timeframe rules
payload.description:
Source backlog key: MVP-02

Normalize user question to structured request

Why
Identity mismatch is the main failure mode

In scope
- Alias config
- regex parse
- timezone defaults
- ambiguity contract

Out of scope
- Full NLP
- multilingual support

Acceptance criteria
- Required prompts parse; these days=14d; this week=local Monday->now; ambiguity returns clarification

Dependencies: MVP-01
Priority: P0
Estimate: 2h
Owner role: Backend / Full-stack
Risk level: High
Demo relevance: Critical
Suggested status: Ready

Artifacts / files expected
- src/query/*.ts
- config/team-members.example.json

Test notes
Parser fixtures for required prompts and ambiguity

Rollback / fallback
Use regex-plus-alias only

payload.subject: [MVP-03] Jira Cloud adapter
payload.description:
Source backlog key: MVP-03

Fetch assigned Jira work and recent updates

Why
Jira is authoritative for assigned work but permission-sensitive

In scope
- Alias->accountId
- user search
- enhanced JQL
- fields extraction
- top-5 changelogs

Out of scope
- Comments
- worklogs
- bulk sync

Acceptance criteria
- Normalized issues returned; enhanced search only; privacy/no-match handled; changelog cap enforced

Dependencies: MVP-01, MVP-02
Priority: P0
Estimate: 3h
Owner role: Integrations
Risk level: High
Demo relevance: Critical
Suggested status: Ready

Artifacts / files expected
- src/adapters/jira.ts
- src/types/jira.ts
- fixtures/jira/*.json

Test notes
Mock success|no-match|cap|permission failure

Rollback / fallback
Skip changelog enrichment if needed

payload.subject: [MVP-04] GitHub commits and recent-repo adapter
payload.description:
Source backlog key: MVP-04

Collect recent commits across tracked repos

Why
Bounded repo scope is the pragmatic MVP

In scope
- Repo iteration
- list commits by author+since
- normalize
- derive repo set

Out of scope
- Global repo discovery
- diff analysis

Acceptance criteria
- Commits come from configured repos only; normalized records include repo/SHA/date/message; repo set deduped

Dependencies: MVP-01, MVP-02
Priority: P0
Estimate: 2.5h
Owner role: Integrations
Risk level: High
Demo relevance: High
Suggested status: Ready

Artifacts / files expected
- src/adapters/github-commits.ts
- fixtures/github/commits/*.json

Test notes
Commit hit|no commits|inaccessible repo|identity noise

Rollback / fallback
Reduce repo count or derive repos from PRs

payload.subject: [MVP-05] GitHub pull-request adapter
payload.description:
Source backlog key: MVP-05

Collect open and recent PRs across tracked repos

Why
PRs are the clearest demo artifact

In scope
- Repo iteration
- list PRs state=all sort=updated
- author/timeframe filtering
- normalize

Out of scope
- Review comments
- merge queue state

Acceptance criteria
- Open and recent PRs returned separately; client-side author filtering documented; empty results are valid

Dependencies: MVP-01, MVP-02
Priority: P0
Estimate: 2h
Owner role: Integrations
Risk level: High
Demo relevance: High
Suggested status: Ready

Artifacts / files expected
- src/adapters/github-prs.ts
- fixtures/github/prs/*.json

Test notes
Open PR|recent closed PR|no PRs|permission miss

Rollback / fallback
Show open PRs only if needed

operations.subject: [MVP-06] Activity schema orchestrator retries and redacted logging

payload.subject: [MVP-06] Activity schema orchestrator retries and redacted logging
payload.description:
Source backlog key: MVP-06

Combine provider data into one resilient payload

Why
Demo should survive single-provider failures

In scope
- Canonical schema
- parallel fan-out
- timeouts
- retry/backoff
- partial-failure flags
- Pino logs

Out of scope
- Distributed tracing
- persistent cache

Acceptance criteria
- Parallel fetch works; one retry on 429/5xx; partial success still yields payload; logs redact secrets

Dependencies: MVP-03, MVP-04, MVP-05
Priority: P0
Estimate: 2.5h
Owner role: Backend / Platform
Risk level: High
Demo relevance: Critical
Suggested status: Ready

Artifacts / files expected
- src/orchestrator/activity.ts
- src/types/activity.ts
- src/lib/logger.ts

Test notes
Partial failure|timeout|retry|redaction assertions

Rollback / fallback
Disable retries and keep single-attempt calls

payload.subject: [MVP-07] Grounded response contract and renderer
payload.description:
Source backlog key: MVP-07

Render ActivitySummary into clear answer

Why
Answer quality matters but must stay bounded

In scope
- Deterministic renderer
- section contract
- caveat rules
- optional LLM polish from normalized facts only

Out of scope
- Chat memory
- tool loops
- free-form agenting

Acceptance criteria
- Renderer works without OpenAI; output includes Overview/Jira/GitHub/Caveats; no unknown evidence mentioned

Dependencies: MVP-06
Priority: P0
Estimate: 1.5h
Owner role: Backend / AI
Risk level: Medium
Demo relevance: Critical
Suggested status: Ready

Artifacts / files expected
- src/render/response.ts
- src/prompts/*.md
- fixtures/render/*.json

Test notes
Grounding snapshots and golden outputs

Rollback / fallback
Template-only mode

operations.subject: [MVP-08] Web chat UI and thin CLI fallback

payload.subject: [MVP-08] Web chat UI and thin CLI fallback
payload.description:
Source backlog key: MVP-08

Expose same flow through web and CLI

Why
Web is for judging; CLI is insurance

In scope
- Static page
- submit flow
- loading states
- CLI wrapper

Out of scope
- React SPA
- user auth
- history persistence

Acceptance criteria
- Required prompts run in web UI; readable error states; CLI prints same answer body

Dependencies: MVP-06, MVP-07
Priority: P0
Estimate: 2h
Owner role: Frontend / Full-stack
Risk level: Medium
Demo relevance: Critical
Suggested status: Ready

Artifacts / files expected
- public/index.html
- public/app.js
- src/cli.ts

Test notes
Manual UI walkthrough and one CLI smoke

Rollback / fallback
Ship web first and keep CLI minimal

operations.subject: [MVP-09] Fixtures contract tests grounding checks and smoke evals

payload.subject: [MVP-09] Fixtures contract tests grounding checks and smoke evals
payload.description:
Source backlog key: MVP-09

Validate with stable fixtures and focused evals

Why
A 2-day prototype needs fast trustworthy tests

In scope
- Sanitized fixtures
- parser tests
- adapter contracts
- grounding checks
- e2e smoke

Out of scope
- Full test matrix
- CI/CD

Acceptance criteria
- Required prompts plus ambiguity/no-activity/not-found/partial-failure covered; golden outputs stored; grounding failures detected

Dependencies: MVP-02, MVP-03, MVP-04, MVP-05, MVP-06, MVP-07, MVP-08
Priority: P1
Estimate: 2h
Owner role: QA / Full-stack
Risk level: Medium
Demo relevance: High
Suggested status: Ready

Artifacts / files expected
- fixtures/*
- tests/*
- evals/*

Test notes
Include at least one recorded fallback run

Rollback / fallback
Rehearse smoke checks manually

operations.subject: [MVP-10] README demo script fallback dataset and tracker export pack

payload.subject: [MVP-10] README demo script fallback dataset and tracker export pack
payload.description:
Source backlog key: MVP-10

Package the build for review and demo

Why
Reviewer experience matters nearly as much as code

In scope
- Setup docs
- sample env docs
- recorded fallback dataset
- 10-minute talk track
- markdown/CSV/JSON backlog exports

Out of scope
- Cloud deploy guide
- direct Jira import automation

Acceptance criteria
- Reviewer can configure in under 15 min; fallback mode answers canned queries without live APIs; ticket pack ready for manual/API recreation

Dependencies: MVP-01, MVP-08, MVP-09
Priority: P1
Estimate: 1.5h
Owner role: PM / Presenter / Full-stack
Risk level: Low
Demo relevance: Critical
Suggested status: Ready

Artifacts / files expected
- README.md
- planning/demo-script.md
- planning/backlog.csv
- planning/backlog.json
- fixtures/demo/*

Test notes
Dry-run setup and fallback rehearsal

Rollback / fallback
Disclose fixture mode if live APIs are unstable

operations.subject: [STR-01] TTL cache and provider concurrency tuning

payload.subject: [STR-01] TTL cache and provider concurrency tuning
payload.description:
Source backlog key: STR-01

Add short cache and bounded fan-out

Why
Helpful for repeated demo queries not required for MVP

In scope
- 5-minute cache
- repo concurrency cap

Out of scope
- Distributed cache
- persistence

Acceptance criteria
- Repeated queries hit cache; repo fan-out bounded; stale cache expires quickly

Dependencies: MVP-04, MVP-05, MVP-06
Priority: P3
Estimate: 1h
Owner role: Backend / Platform
Risk level: Low
Demo relevance: Medium
Suggested status: Icebox

Artifacts / files expected
- src/lib/cache.ts
- src/lib/concurrency.ts

Test notes
One cache-hit smoke and one concurrency-limited run

Rollback / fallback
Keep direct live calls

operations.subject: [STR-02] Interactive member disambiguation and repo-coverage helper

payload.subject: [STR-02] Interactive member disambiguation and repo-coverage helper
payload.description:
Source backlog key: STR-02

Improve ambiguous-name UX and visible repo scope

Why
Useful polish if demo data has multiple Johns

In scope
- Disambiguation prompt
- repo coverage panel

Out of scope
- Full admin UI
- dynamic repo discovery

Acceptance criteria
- Ambiguous names return choices; tracked repos visible from config

Dependencies: MVP-02, MVP-08
Priority: P3
Estimate: 1.5h
Owner role: Frontend / Full-stack
Risk level: Medium
Demo relevance: Medium
Suggested status: Icebox

Artifacts / files expected
- public/*
- src/query/identity.ts

Test notes
One ambiguous-user fixture

Rollback / fallback
Keep text-only clarification message
